pattern: Integer division without the divide operator is repeated doubling-and-subtraction of the divisor, with the quotient clamped to the signed 32-bit range.
hints: You may not use multiplication, division, or mod, and the result truncates toward zero, so think in terms of subtraction.
Subtracting the divisor one at a time is too slow; subtract the largest doubling of the divisor that still fits, like long division in binary.
Work with absolute values, repeatedly subtract divisor << k for the largest k that fits and add 1 << k to the quotient, fix the sign, then clamp to [-2^31, 2^31 - 1].

edge_inputs.description: INT_MIN divided by -1 overflows and must clamp
INT_MIN divided by 1 stays in range
negative dividend truncates toward zero not down
divisor larger than dividend yields zero
INT_MIN divided by INT_MIN is one

stress.description: random 32-bit dividend and nonzero divisor across all sign combinations
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    LO, HI = -2147483648, 2147483647
    dividend = rng.randint(LO, HI)
    divisor = 0
    while divisor == 0:
        divisor = rng.randint(LO, HI)
    return [dividend, divisor]
stress.note: divisor forced nonzero per constraints; full 32-bit range exercises the clamp
